core_mechanism: Counting-based additive-superposition scaling: expressing an aggregate observable as an accumulation over a counted number of independent pairwise interaction events, then testing whether it grows linearly with that count (additive superposition of independent events) or saturates to a bounded per-event contribution
brief_reason: The abstract studies yields as a function of the number of binary nucleon-nucleon collisions v and reports that the yield per collision saturates with v for high-momentum particles, i.e. a test of linear-in-count independent-event superposition versus a saturating per-event increment.
